message:
fix(resources): preserve source order within same precedence tier

precedence_sorted_enabled_paths() previously used a full comparator
that broke ties by lexicographic path comparison, which meant
CLI-specified extension ordering was silently rewritten to alphabetical
order within the same precedence tier. This made the order in which a
user listed extensions on the command line behaviorally insignificant.

Switches from sort_by() with a tiebreaker to sort_by_key() on
precedence alone, which is a stable sort and preserves the original
insertion order for resources at the same precedence level. Users
can now rely on extension ordering to control resource shadowing.

Adds test asserting that two Temporary-scope resources (zeta before
alpha) retain their source order after sorting, while a Project-scope
resource sorts after both.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
